column1: # Mapping from GLEAM feed codes to model entities
#   - gleam_code: GLEAM feed material code (from Tables 3.1 and 3.5)

column5: Hay or silage from grass (same as GRASSF for model)
Hay from adjacent areas (same as GRASSF)
Approximate match though sorghum has lower nitrogen content than assumed for FDDRSIL
Leaves from trees/forest (not modeled for now)
Barley and other cereals (using barley as representative)
Oat grain represented by GLEAM mixed cereals nutritional profile
Rye grain represented by GLEAM mixed cereals nutritional profile
Buckwheat grain represented by GLEAM mixed cereals nutritional profile
Oat grain proxied with barley nutritional profile
Rye grain proxied with barley nutritional profile
Buckwheat grain proxied with barley nutritional profile

Whole groundnuts (peanuts) as monogastric feed; SOY proxy is appropriate as groundnut and soybean both supply high-protein, high-fat oilseed feed material. Real-world flow: lower-grade or unshelled peanuts in West Africa/India go directly to pig and poultry feed without oil pressing.
Legume grains → dry pea as representative
Legume grains → phaseolus bean (cull/broken beans + whole-bean inclusion as monogastric protein supplement; FBS books a small Feed share for item 2546)
Legume grains → chickpea (minor monogastric feed; FBS Pulses-Other Feed element)
Legume grains → gram (Bengal gram = chickpea; same minor feed role)
Potato tubers as pig feed (CASSAVA nutritional proxy)
Sweet potato roots as pig feed (CASSAVA nutritional proxy)
Soybean meal → oilseed meal (generic oilseed meal pool)
Palm kernel cake — byproduct of palm kernel oil extraction (Feedipedia: ~17 % CP, ~5–12 % residual oil, high fibre). Modelled via the palm_oil pathway in foods.csv at 0.022 t PKM per t FFB.
Dry grain byproducts (brans, middlings) → wheat bran
DDGS from grain ethanol production (wet grain byproducts)
Corn gluten meal byproduct of wet-milling, ~60 % CP. Produced by the maize_wetmill pathway.
Corn gluten feed byproduct of wet-milling, ~21 % CP. Produced by the maize_wetmill pathway.
Sugar beet pulp byproduct of sucrose extraction (~8 % CP, high fibre). Produced by the sugarbeet_sugar pathway at 0.05 t dry pulp per t fresh beet.
Calcium source for laying hens - not modeled
